days.title: Week 5, Day 1: The Letter Ö
days.cultural_note: Congratulations on reaching the final letter of the Finnish alphabet! 'Ö' is a front rounded vowel, crucial for vowel harmony.
days.pronunciation_tip: This sound is like the 'i' in 'sir' or the 'u' in 'fur'. Round your lips as if for 'O', but make the 'er' sound from inside your mouth.

days.title: Week 5, Day 2: Alphabet Review
days.cultural_note: Repetition is key to mastering a new alphabet. Take time to review all the letters you've learned.
days.pronunciation_tip: Listen to Finnish speakers and try to mimic the sounds. Pay close attention to the vowels.

days.title: Week 5, Day 3: Vowel Harmony
days.cultural_note: Vowel harmony is a core rule of Finnish. Back vowels (a, o, u) cannot appear in the same native word as front vowels (ä, ö, y). Vowels 'e' and 'i' are neutral and can go with either group.
days.pronunciation_tip: Understanding vowel harmony will help you correctly guess the endings of words. For example, talo (back vowels) -> talossa, but metsä (front vowels) -> metsässä.

days.title: Week 5, Day 4: Consonant Gradation
days.cultural_note: Consonant gradation (astevaihtelu) is a change that happens to consonants (k, p, t) in the middle of words when an ending is added. For example, 'katu' (street) becomes 'kadun' (street's).
days.pronunciation_tip: You don't need to master this rule now, but being aware of it will help you understand why words seem to change their spelling.

days.title: Week 5, Day 5: Long Vowels & Consonants
days.cultural_note: In Finnish, double letters are pronounced for twice as long as single letters. This is very important and changes the meaning of words. 'tuli' = fire, 'tuuli' = wind, 'tulli' = customs.
days.pronunciation_tip: When you see a double letter, hold the sound for a longer duration. For vowels, it's like singing the note for a bit longer.

days.title: Week 5, Day 6: Practice Reading
days.cultural_note: Find a simple Finnish text, like a children's book or a news headline, and try to read it aloud. Don't worry about understanding, just focus on the sounds.
days.pronunciation_tip: Remember, every letter is pronounced! There are no silent letters in Finnish.

days.title: Week 5, Day 7: Congratulations!
days.cultural_note: You have learned the building blocks of the Finnish language. This is a huge achievement and the foundation for everything else you will learn.
days.pronunciation_tip: Keep practicing the sounds, especially the ones that are new to you like Y, Ä, and Ö. You're ready to start the Survival Path!
